title: Making Differentiation Media for SH-SY5Y
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"2knu1","text":"This describes how to make differentiation medium for SH-SY5Y using all-trans-retinoic acid.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":72,"length":6}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: In a 15 mL centrifuge tube, add 14.25 mL ethanol and 0.75 mL autoclaved deionized water. This will make a 95% ethanol solution.
Weigh out 5 mg ATRA. ATRA is light sensitive, so do this step with the lights off.
Transfer 3330 µL of 95% EtOH solution to another 15 mL centrifuge tube.
Add the 5 mg ATRA to the 3330 µL 95% EtOH solution. This makes a 5 mM stock ATRA solution.
Vortex mix the solution.

This solution is used at a rate of 100 µL stock solution per 50 mL differentiation media. The final concentration should be 10 μM. We most recently made 10 aliquots, each with 62 µL . This is to make 10 30 mL media batches, each using 60 µL .
Stock solution can be stored for up to 6 weeks at 4 °C .
Weigh out 175 mg l-glutamine.
Aliquot 15 mL NBM into a 15 mL centrifuge tube.
Add the l-glutamine to the NBM.
Vortex mix the solution.

Aliquot the solution into 1.5 mL aliquots. This solution is 20x. The concentration is 80 mM and the final desired concentration is 4 mM. This protocol makes 10 aliquots of 1.5 mL . Each stock aliquot makes 30 mL differentiation medium.
Store aliquots at -20 °C .
Aliquot 600 µL batches of 50x B-27 supplement.
Each aliquot will make 30 mL differentiation media.
Do not mix the media until you are ready to change the media in a flask. It degrades quickly. Warm 30 mL NBM in water bath.
Thaw one 50x B-27 supplement aliquot, and one 20x l-glutamine supplement.
Store aliquots at -20 °C .

In biosafety cabinet, combine the following in a 50 mL tube 29.34 mL Nuerobasal Medium 600 µL 50x B-27 Supplement 1.5 mL 20x l-glutamine Stock Solution 60 µL 500x All- trans- retinoic Acid Stock Solution
Sterile filter the differentiation media.
Use immediately.
creator: Kenneth Schackart
